data.Modification: Iodoacetamide derivative/Addition of Glycine/Addition of G

project.title: RPK1 phosphorylation and cell death
project.projectDescription: The molecular mechanisms regulating the switch between RIPK1 pro-survival and pro-death functions remain poorly understood. We identified Ikkα/β-mediated phosphorylation of RIPK1 as a key mechanism inhibiting RIPK1 kinase activity and preventing TNF-mediated RIPK1 kinase-dependent cell death.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: 5.10^6 wild-type or Ikkα/β-/- MEFs were stimulated for 5 minutes with 2µg/ml FLAG-hTNF and then immunoprecipated according to the FLAG IP protocol. After the final wash step in NP-40 lysis buffer, the beads were additionally washed for 5 times in ice-cold PBS. Protein complexes were then eluted from the beads by addition of 200µL 150µg/ml 3x FLAG-peptide in PBS, followed by incubation for 30 min at 22°C.  The eluate was then separated from the beads by applying the samples over Mobicol columns with a 35µM pore size. Proteins were reduced, alkylated and digested using the FASP protocol. Digestion was performed with LysC and trypsin at 37 °C overnight. Finally, the sample was acidified with formic acid to a final concentration of 5%. Tryptic peptides were desalted using Sep-Pak C18 cartridges, dried in vacuo and stored at -80 °C. Phospho-peptides enrichment was performed using Ti4+-IMAC beads. RP nanoLC-MS/MS was performed using an UHPLC 1290 system coupled to an Orbitrap Q Exactive Plus mass spectrometer. Peptides were first trapped (Dr Maisch Reprosil C18, 3 μm, 2 cm x 100 μm i.d.) before being separated on an analytical column (Agilent Poroshell EC-C18, 2.7 μm, 50 cm x 75 μm i.d.). Trapping was performed for 10 min in solvent A (0.1% FA acid in water, at 5µ mL/min) and the gradient was as follows: 13-41% solvent B (0.1 % FA in 80% ACN) in 35 min, 41-100% in 3 min, and finally 100% for 1 min before returning in 1 min to 100% solvent A until the end of the run. Total analysis time was 60 min. The mass spectrometer was operated in data-dependent mode. Full-scan MS spectra from m/z 375 to m/z 1600 were acquired at a resolution of 35,000 at m/z 400, after accumulation to a target value of 3e6. Up to 10 most intense precursor ions were selected for fragmentation. HCD fragmentation was performed at normalized collision energy of 25 after the accumulation to a target value of 5e4 and a maximum injection time of 120 ms. MS/MS spectra were acquired at a resolution of 17,500. Dynamic exclusion was set to 10 s.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw data were processed with MaxQuant version 1.5.3.28. The MS/MS spectra were searched against a Swissprot Mus Musculus database version 2015_10 (16,727 entries) using the Andromeda search engine. The database search was performed with the following parameters: an initial mass tolerance of ±20 ppm and a final mass tolerance of ±4.5 ppm for precursor masses, ±20 ppm for HCD Orbitrap fragment ions, enzyme specificity was set to trypsin allowing up to two missed cleavages. Cysteine carbamidomethylation was used as a fixed modification, whereas methionine oxidation, protein N-terminal acetylation and serine, threonine and tyrosine phosphorylation as variable modifications. The false discovery rate was set to 0.01 for peptides, proteins and phosphosites. A site localization probability of at least 0.75 was used as threshold for the localization of phosphoresidues.
